article_title: China Launches Shenzhou 23 With Hong Kong's First Astronaut as One Crew Member Prepares for Historic Year-Long Stay
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: China Launches Shenzhou 23 With Hong Kong's First Astronaut as One Crew Member Prepares for Historic Year-Long Stay
content_preview.summary: Shenzhou 23 lifted off May 24 carrying Lai Ka-ying, Hong Kong's first astronaut, with one of the three crew members set for China's first year-long spaceflight.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

China launched its Shenzhou 23 crewed spacecraft on Sunday, May 24, 2026, carrying three astronauts to the Tiangong space station — including Lai Ka-ying, the first astronaut from Hong Kong to reach space. One of the three crew members is set to remain aboard Tiangong for approximately one year, marking China's first year-long spaceflight, according to [Space.com](https://www.space.com/space-exploration/human-spaceflight/china-shenzhou-23-astronaut-launch-tiangong-space-station).

#...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article with clear sections covering the crew, year-long mission milestone, spacecraft upgrades, and the prior Shenzhou 21 crew's extended stay. Word count (649) is within the 400–1200 range for News. The 'What We Don't Know' section is a commendable editorial choice that acknowledges open questions without speculation.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 4 source snapshots read from disk. source-0.html.gz (Space.com): confirmed launch time (11:08 a.m. EDT / 1508 GMT), crew names, year-long mission detail, Shenzhou 24 Pakistani astronaut context, Shenzhou 21 crew names (Zhang Lu, Wu Fei, Zhang Hongzhang), May 29 return date, and Shenzhou 22 lifeboat narrative. source-1.html.gz (PBS NewsHour / AP): confirmed crew roster, Lai Ka-ying as first Hong Kong astronaut, doctoral degree in computer forensics, year-long mission purpose ('explore human adaptability and performance limits'), 'among the world's longest single stays in space', dozens of science projects. source-2.html.gz (Global Times): confirmed Zhu Yangzhu as commander and third-batch astronaut who flew on Shenzhou-16, Zhang Zhiyuan as third-batch and PLA Air Force pilot, Lai Ka-ying as fourth-batch and first from Hong Kong, 3.5-hour rapid rendezvous claim, payload capacity increase from 50 kg to over 100 kg, volume expanded to three times previous capacity. QUOTE ISSUE: The article renders Lai's pre-launch statement as: '"As an ordinary person from Hong Kong, being able to join the astronaut team...is an opportunity I never dared to dream of"' — the Global Times source records the full quote as: '"As an ordinary person from Hong Kong, being able to join the astronaut team and be selected for this mission is an opportunity I never dared to dream of. My heart is full of gratitude and honor."' The ellipsis elides 'and be selected for this mission' and the closing sentence is dropped entirely. This is a paraphrase inside quotation marks, recoverable as a corrections note. source-3.html.gz (WDIO / AP wire): confirmed crew roster, Lai background, year-long mission detail, and science projects count.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All major claims verified against snapshots. Crew composition, roles, batch assignments, launch time and location, year-long mission mechanics (Shenzhou 24 / Pakistani astronaut scheduling), spacecraft upgrade specs (50 kg → over 100 kg payload, 3× volume), and the Shenzhou 21 crew situation all confirmed. One quote truncation identified (see source_verification).
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism, no AI self-reference, no editorializing. The framing of China's space achievements is factual and restrained.
editor_notes.originality: No duplicate found. A prior article covers the Tianzhou-10 cargo mission (2026-05-13); this submission covers the separate Shenzhou 23 crewed mission, a distinct event.
concerns: Lai Ka-ying's pre-launch quote is rendered with an ellipsis that replaces 'and be selected for this mission' and omits the closing sentence 'My heart is full of gratitude and honor.' The quote as printed implies verbatim text but is a selective truncation. This should be documented in a corrections record.

globaltimes.cn and wdio.com are not in the source allowlist. Global Times is Chinese state media; its inclusion is factually appropriate for official Chinese space program reporting. WDIO carries AP wire copy, which is a reputable primary source. Both are editorially acceptable for this story.
recommendations: Add wdio.com (AP wire republisher) to the source allowlist, or cite the AP directly.
The quote truncation is filed as a correction — no action needed on the article itself.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong submission covering a significant space news event. All central claims are sourced and verified. The single issue — a paraphrased quote presented inside quotation marks — is recoverable with a corrections note. APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS.
